zotero.abstractNote: The reconstruction of non-rigid 3D scenes from monocular or multi-view video is a fundamental problem in computer vision and computer graphics. While traditional methods relied on template-based approaches or strong priors, the field has recently witnessed a paradigm shift towards template-free, data-driven, and neural representations. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of the recent trends in 3D reconstruction of general non-rigid scenes. We categorize existing methods based on their underlying representation, such as point-based, mesh-based, and volumetric representations, and discuss their strengths and limitations. Furthermore, we analyze the role of different supervision signals, including multi-view consistency, photometric loss, and learned priors. Finally, we identify open challenges and promising future research directions in this rapidly evolving field.
